---
title: "Operaciones geomĂ©tricas con datos vectoriales"
author: "Dr. Francisco Zambrano"
format: 
  revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    #title-slide-attributes: 
      #data-background-image: "https://media.giphy.com/media/GldXiKXeO7hpczEHxt/giphy.gif"
      #data-background-size: "contain"
    title-block-banner: true
    logo: "logo_hemera.jpg"
    footer: "IMSE1017|2-2022"
    theme: default
    echo: true
---
<<<<<<< HEAD

## Contenidos {.smaller .scrollable background-image="https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/520851/34887433-ce1d130e-f7c6-11e7-83fc-d60ad4fae6bd.gif" background-position="97.5% 2.5%" background-size="7.5%"}

Operaciones geomĂ©tricas con datos vectoriales:

1. SimplificaciĂ³n
2. Centroides
3. Buffers
4. Clipping (recortar)
5. Union

=======
>>>>>>> refs/remotes/origin/gh-pages
```{r setup}
#| echo: false
#| message: false
library(sf)
library(terra)
library(spData)
```

<<<<<<< HEAD
## 1. SimplificaciĂ³n {.smaller .scrollable background-image="https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/520851/34887433-ce1d130e-f7c6-11e7-83fc-d60ad4fae6bd.gif" background-position="97.5% 2.5%" background-size="7.5%"}

La `simplificaciĂ³n` es un proceso de generalizaciĂ³n de objetos vectoriales (lĂ­neas y polĂ­gonos) generalmente para su uso en mapas de menor escala.
=======
## Contenidos {.smaller .scrollable}

Operaciones geomĂ©tricas con datos vectoriales:

1. SimplificaciĂ³n
2. Centroides
3. Buffers
4. Clipping (recortar)
5. Cortar y subconjunto
6. Union

## SimplificaciĂ³n {.smaller .scrollable}

La simplificaciĂ³n es un proceso de generalizaciĂ³n de objetos vectoriales (lĂ­neas y polĂ­gonos) generalmente para su uso en mapas de menor escala.
>>>>>>> refs/remotes/origin/gh-pages

### `geometria:` linea

```{r}

seine_simp = st_simplify(seine, dTolerance = 2000)  # 2000 m
```
<br>

::: columns

::: {.column width="50%"}
```{r}
#| out.width = "120%"
plot(seine$geometry)
```
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
```{r}
plot(seine_simp$geometry)
```
:::
:::

<<<<<<< HEAD
## 1. SimplificaciĂ³n  {.smaller .scrollable background-image="https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/520851/34887433-ce1d130e-f7c6-11e7-83fc-d60ad4fae6bd.gif" background-position="97.5% 2.5%" background-size="7.5%"}
=======
## 1. SimplificaciĂ³n  {.smaller .scrollable}
>>>>>>> refs/remotes/origin/gh-pages

El objeto `seine_simp` resultante es una copia del `seine` original pero con menos vĂ©rtices. Esto es evidente, siendo el resultado visualmente mĂ¡s simple y consumiendo menos memoria que el objeto original, como se verifica a continuaciĂ³n:

```{r}
object.size(seine)
object.size(seine_simp)
```


<<<<<<< HEAD
## 1. SimplificaciĂ³n  {.smaller .scrollable background-image="https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/520851/34887433-ce1d130e-f7c6-11e7-83fc-d60ad4fae6bd.gif" background-position="97.5% 2.5%" background-size="7.5%"}
=======
## 1. SimplificaciĂ³n  {.smaller .scrollable}
>>>>>>> refs/remotes/origin/gh-pages

La simplificaciĂ³n es un proceso de generalizaciĂ³n de objetos vectoriales (lĂ­neas y polĂ­gonos) generalmente para su uso en mapas de menor escala.

### `geometria:` polĂ­gono

```{r}
us_states2163 = st_transform(us_states, "EPSG:2163")
us_states_simp1 = st_simplify(us_states2163, dTolerance = 100000)  # 100 km
```

::: columns

::: {.column width="50%"}
```{r}
#| out.width = "120%"
plot(us_states2163$geometry)
object.size(us_states2163)
```
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
```{r}
plot(us_states_simp1$geometry)
object.size(us_states_simp1)
```
:::
:::

<<<<<<< HEAD
## 2. Centroides  {.smaller .scrollable background-image="https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/520851/34887433-ce1d130e-f7c6-11e7-83fc-d60ad4fae6bd.gif" background-position="97.5% 2.5%" background-size="7.5%"}
=======
## 2. Centroides  {.smaller .scrollable}
>>>>>>> refs/remotes/origin/gh-pages

Las operaciones de centroide identifican el centro de los objetos geogrĂ¡ficos.

La funciĂ³n `st_centroid` de {sf} permite calcular los centroides.

```{r}
nz_centroid = st_centroid(nz)
seine_centroid = st_centroid(seine)
```

<<<<<<< HEAD
## 2. Centroides  {.smaller .scrollable background-image="https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/520851/34887433-ce1d130e-f7c6-11e7-83fc-d60ad4fae6bd.gif" background-position="97.5% 2.5%" background-size="7.5%"}
=======
## 2. Centroides  {.smaller .scrollable}
>>>>>>> refs/remotes/origin/gh-pages

Las operaciones de centroide identifican el centro de los objetos geogrĂ¡ficos.

```{r}
plot(nz$geom)
plot(nz_centroid$geom,add = TRUE,col='red')
```
<<<<<<< HEAD
## 2. Centroides {.smaller .scrollable background-image="https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/520851/34887433-ce1d130e-f7c6-11e7-83fc-d60ad4fae6bd.gif" background-position="97.5% 2.5%" background-size="7.5%"}
=======
## 2. Centroides {.smaller .scrollable}
>>>>>>> refs/remotes/origin/gh-pages

Las operaciones de centroide identifican el centro de los objetos geogrĂ¡ficos.
```{r}
plot(seine$geometry)
plot(seine_centroid$geom,add = TRUE,col = 'red')
```

<<<<<<< HEAD
## 2. Centroides  {.smaller .scrollable background-image="https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/520851/34887433-ce1d130e-f7c6-11e7-83fc-d60ad4fae6bd.gif" background-position="97.5% 2.5%" background-size="7.5%"}
=======
## 2. Centroides  {.smaller .scrollable}
>>>>>>> refs/remotes/origin/gh-pages

A veces, el centroide geogrĂ¡fico cae fuera de los lĂ­mites de sus objetos principales (piense en una dona).

La funciĂ³n `st_point_on_surface` de `{sf}` asegura que los puntos caeran en el objeto.

```{r}
#| out.width = '6cm'
nz_pos = st_point_on_surface(nz)
plot(nz$geom)
plot(nz_pos$geom,add = TRUE,col = 'red')
```

<<<<<<< HEAD
## 2. Centroides  {.smaller .scrollable background-image="https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/520851/34887433-ce1d130e-f7c6-11e7-83fc-d60ad4fae6bd.gif" background-position="97.5% 2.5%" background-size="7.5%"}
=======
## 2. Centroides  {.smaller .scrollable}
>>>>>>> refs/remotes/origin/gh-pages

A veces, el centroide geogrĂ¡fico cae fuera de los lĂ­mites de sus objetos principales (piense en una dona).

La funciĂ³n `st_point_on_surface` de `{sf}` asegura que los puntos caeran en el objeto.

```{r}
seine_pos = st_point_on_surface(seine)
plot(seine$geom)
plot(seine_pos$geom,add = TRUE,col = 'red')
```

<<<<<<< HEAD
## 3. Buffers  {.smaller .scrollable background-image="https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/520851/34887433-ce1d130e-f7c6-11e7-83fc-d60ad4fae6bd.gif" background-position="97.5% 2.5%" background-size="7.5%"}
=======
## 3. Buffers  {.smaller .scrollable}
>>>>>>> refs/remotes/origin/gh-pages

Los `buffer` (zonas de influencia) son polĂ­gonos que representan el Ă¡rea dentro de una distancia determinada de una entidad geomĂ©trica: independientemente de si la entrada es un punto, una lĂ­nea o un polĂ­gono, la salida es un polĂ­gono.

```{r}
seine_buff_5km = st_buffer(seine, dist = 5000)
plot(seine_buff_5km$geometry,col ='blue')
plot(seine$geometry,add = TRUE)
```

<<<<<<< HEAD
## 3. Buffers  {.smaller .scrollable background-image="https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/520851/34887433-ce1d130e-f7c6-11e7-83fc-d60ad4fae6bd.gif" background-position="97.5% 2.5%" background-size="7.5%"}
=======
## 3. Buffers  {.smaller .scrollable}
>>>>>>> refs/remotes/origin/gh-pages

Los `buffer` (zonas de influencia) son polĂ­gonos que representan el Ă¡rea dentro de una distancia determinada de una entidad geomĂ©trica: independientemente de si la entrada es un punto, una lĂ­nea o un polĂ­gono, la salida es un polĂ­gono.

```{r}
seine_buff_50km = st_buffer(seine, dist = 50000)
plot(seine_buff_50km$geometry,col ='blue')
plot(seine$geometry,add = TRUE)
```

<<<<<<< HEAD
## 4. Recortar (clipping) {.smaller .scrollable background-image="https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/520851/34887433-ce1d130e-f7c6-11e7-83fc-d60ad4fae6bd.gif" background-position="97.5% 2.5%" background-size="7.5%"}

El recorte espacial es una forma de creaciĂ³n de subconjuntos espaciales que implica cambios en las columnas de geometrĂ­a de al menos algunas de las entidades afectadas.

![](../img/venn-clip-1.png){width="800px"}

## 4. Recortar (clipping) {.smaller .scrollable background-image="https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/520851/34887433-ce1d130e-f7c6-11e7-83fc-d60ad4fae6bd.gif" background-position="97.5% 2.5%" background-size="7.5%"}

Un ejemplo utilizando `sf`

```{r}
library(sf)
b = st_sfc(st_point(c(0, 1)), st_point(c(1, 1))) # create 2 points
b = st_buffer(b, dist = 1) # convert points to circles
plot(b, border = "grey")
text(x = c(-0.5, 1.5), y = 1, labels = c("x", "y"), cex = 3) # add text
```

## 4. Recortar (clipping) {.smaller .scrollable background-image="https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/520851/34887433-ce1d130e-f7c6-11e7-83fc-d60ad4fae6bd.gif" background-position="97.5% 2.5%" background-size="7.5%"}

**Diferencia:** `st_difference`

```{r}
x = b[1]
y = b[2]
x_diff_y = st_difference(x, y)
plot(b, border = "grey")
plot(x_diff_y, col = "lightgrey", border = "grey", add = TRUE)
```

## 4. Recortar (clipping) {.smaller .scrollable background-image="https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/520851/34887433-ce1d130e-f7c6-11e7-83fc-d60ad4fae6bd.gif" background-position="97.5% 2.5%" background-size="7.5%"}

**Diferencia:** `st_difference`

```{r}
y_diff_x = st_difference(y, x)
plot(b, border = "grey")
plot(y_diff_x, col = "lightgrey", border = "grey", add = TRUE)
```

## 4. Recortar (clipping) {.smaller .scrollable background-image="https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/520851/34887433-ce1d130e-f7c6-11e7-83fc-d60ad4fae6bd.gif" background-position="97.5% 2.5%" background-size="7.5%"}

**UniĂ³n:** `st_union`

```{r}
y_union_x = st_union(y, x)
plot(b, border = "grey")
plot(y_union_x, col = "lightgrey", border = "grey", add = TRUE)
```

## 4. Recortar (clipping) {.smaller .scrollable background-image="https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/520851/34887433-ce1d130e-f7c6-11e7-83fc-d60ad4fae6bd.gif" background-position="97.5% 2.5%" background-size="7.5%"}

**IntersecciĂ³n:** `st_intersection`

```{r}
x_and_y = st_intersection(x, y)
plot(b, border = "grey")
plot(x_and_y, col = "lightgrey", border = "grey", add = TRUE)
```

## 4. Recortar (clipping) {.smaller .scrollable background-image="https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/520851/34887433-ce1d130e-f7c6-11e7-83fc-d60ad4fae6bd.gif" background-position="97.5% 2.5%" background-size="7.5%"}

**Diferencia simĂ©trica:** `st_sym_difference`

```{r}
x = b[1]
y = b[2]
x_sym_y = st_sym_difference(x, y)
plot(b, border = "grey")
plot(x_sym_y, col = "lightgrey", border = "grey", add = TRUE)
```

## 5. UniĂ³n (clipping) {.smaller .scrollable background-image="https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/520851/34887433-ce1d130e-f7c6-11e7-83fc-d60ad4fae6bd.gif" background-position="97.5% 2.5%" background-size="7.5%"}

Une geometrias. Funciona de dos formas posibles:

**disuelve**

```{r}
nc <- read_sf(system.file("shape/nc.shp", package="sf"))
plot(nc$geometry)
```
## 5. UniĂ³n (clipping) {.smaller .scrollable background-image="https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/520851/34887433-ce1d130e-f7c6-11e7-83fc-d60ad4fae6bd.gif" background-position="97.5% 2.5%" background-size="7.5%"}

Une geometrias. Funciona de dos formas posibles:

**disuelve**

```{r}
plot(st_union(nc$geometry))
```

## 5. UniĂ³n (clipping) {.smaller .scrollable background-image="https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/520851/34887433-ce1d130e-f7c6-11e7-83fc-d60ad4fae6bd.gif" background-position="97.5% 2.5%" background-size="7.5%"}

Une geometrias. Funciona de dos formas posibles:

**une**

```{r}
plot(c(nc$geometry[1],nc$geometry[10]))
```

## 5. UniĂ³n (clipping) {.smaller .scrollable background-image="https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/520851/34887433-ce1d130e-f7c6-11e7-83fc-d60ad4fae6bd.gif" background-position="97.5% 2.5%" background-size="7.5%"}

Une geometrias. Funciona de dos formas posibles:

**une**

```{r}
plot(st_union(nc$geometry[1],nc$geometry[10]))
```
## 5. UniĂ³n (clipping) {.smaller .scrollable background-image="https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/520851/34887433-ce1d130e-f7c6-11e7-83fc-d60ad4fae6bd.gif" background-position="97.5% 2.5%" background-size="7.5%"}

Â¿CuĂ¡l es la diferencia?

```{r}
c(nc$geometry[1],nc$geometry[10])
st_union(nc$geometry[1],nc$geometry[10])
```
=======
## 4. Recortar (clipping) {.smaller .scrollable}

El recorte espacial es una forma de creaciĂ³n de subconjuntos espaciales que implica cambios en las columnas de geometrĂ­a de al menos algunas de las entidades afectadas.



![](../img/venn-clip-1.png){width="800px"}
>>>>>>> refs/remotes/origin/gh-pages
